revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | Era = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | Burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | Era = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | Burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | Era = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | Era = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | Era = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] |coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}}

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] {{citation needed|Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] {{citation needed|Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996.
