revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] {{citation needed|Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:Kofun template {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]][[Category:Kofun template Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. |coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft|12=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:山田・宝馬古墳群 up to rev 93901181
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=山田・宝馬古墳群 |画像= |所在地=千葉県山武郡芝山町山田・宝馬 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘全長30-40m |築造年代=6世紀 |出土品=埴輪 |史跡指定= |埋葬施設= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=山田・宝馬
古墳群|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''山田・宝馬古墳群'''（やまだ・ほうまこふんぐん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]にある[[古墳群]]である。 == 概要 == 山田・宝馬古墳群は、芝山町の山田・宝馬地区の[[栗山川]]の支流[[高谷川 (栗山川水系)|高谷川]]と[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]に挟まれた標高40 - 45メートルの[[台地]]上に分布する[[古墳群]]である。 宝馬古墳群には、[[前方後円墳]]4基、[[円墳]]66基、また山田・朝倉古墳群には、前方後円墳7基、円墳157基が存在していたとされていたが既に多くが消滅し、[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）の[[成田国際空港]]付帯工事に伴う調査では山田・宝馬古墳群全体で現存する[[古墳]]は、前方後円墳6基、円墳20基と報告されている。前方後円墳はいずれも墳丘長30 - 40メートルの小規模なものである。 これまで調査された前方後円墳のうち、35号墳（宝馬古墳）は墳丘長30メートルで箱型[[石棺]]を有し、[[鉄鏃]]が出土、墳丘に[[円筒埴輪]]や、人物埴輪・器財埴輪などの[[形象埴輪]]をめぐらせていた。65号墳（瓢塚古墳）は墳丘長35メートルで[[横穴式石室]]を有し、銅鋺・直刀・金銅耳環・棗玉・ガラス玉が出土した。127号墳は、墳丘長34メートル、後円部径21.6メートル、前方部幅21メートルで、残存していた幅5 - 6.5メートルの周堀が確認され、堀の中から多くの人物や動物の形象埴輪、あるいは円筒埴輪が発見された。特に墳丘の東側のくびれ部から前方部にかけて、多くの形象埴輪が立てられていた。164号墳（にわとり塚古墳）は墳丘長40メートルで、くびれ部に箱型石棺を有し、直刀・銀製刀子・鉄鏃・耳環・馬具等が出土、墳丘に円筒埴輪や、人物・家・動物などの形象埴輪をめぐらせていた。これらの[[埴輪]]は[[房総]]の古墳に於ける埴輪配列の重要な例となっている。 円墳のなかには箱型石棺を内部施設とするものが多く認められる。また調査された古墳の大半は[[6世紀]]後半のもので[[群集墳]]的性格が強い。 == 参考文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、1989年。ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[芝山古墳・はにわ博物館]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 展示紹介 山田・宝馬35号墳] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたほうまこふんくん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:芝山町の歴史]]

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]]

Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]]

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|qq=}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shibayama, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[en:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:芝山町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Shibayama, Chiba
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|qq=}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:芝山町の歴史]]

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|qq=}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-shaped stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-shaped stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-shaped stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:芝山町の歴史]]

A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type stone coffin. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[sn:amada-Takaraba Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|qq=|1=Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|qq=|1=Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type stone coffin in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type stone coffins. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}}

Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996.
