[[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.708889|N|140.382778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11470125]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|1=Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|1=Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.708889|N|140.382778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11470125]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|1=Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|1=Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group | image = | location = Yamada and Hōma, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli | area = 30–40 meters in mound length | built = 6th century | burial goods = Haniwa | alternate_name = | burial burials = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group
|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama]], Sanbu District, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|1=Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|1=Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}}

Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum]] == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|1=Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|1=Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]] identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|en|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|WD=Q11614636|lt=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

|coordinates=35|coordinates=42|coordinates=32|lon_deg=140|lon_min=22|lon_sec=58|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|32|N|140|22|58|E}}}} }} '''Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group''' (山田・宝馬古墳群) refers to an assemblage of kofun (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|en|Shibayama, Chiba|WD=Q1203689|lt=Shibayama}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. == Overview == The Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group spans the Yamada and Hōma areas of Shibayama Town. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|1=Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|1=Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}. Historically, the Hōma Kofun Group comprised 4 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 66 round mounds, while the Yamada-Asakura Kofun Group included 7 keyhole-shaped tumuli and 157 round mounds. However, many have since been lost. A survey conducted in 1981 during construction associated with {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}} identified 6 remaining keyhole-shaped tumuli and 20 round mounds across the entire Yamada-Hōma area. The keyhole-shaped tumuli are relatively small, with mound lengths of 30–40 meters. Several of the keyhole-shaped tumuli excavated include: - '''Mound 35 (Hōma Kofun):''' This tumulus, 30 meters in length, features a box-type Sarcophagus. Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|en|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|WD=Q11614636|1=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|WD=Q11614636|lt=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-6yamada.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibition on Yamada-Hōma Mound 35] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|version=93901181|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山田・宝馬古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11470125}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Excavations yielded iron arrowheads, and cylindrical, human-shaped, and artifact-shaped haniwa were discovered arranged around the mound. - '''Mound 65 (Hyōzuka Kofun):''' Measuring 35 meters in length, it contains a horizontal stone chamber. burial goods discovered include a bronze bowl, straight swords, gilded earrings, beads of natsume and glass, and other items. - '''Mound 127:''' With a mound length of 34 meters, a rear-circle diameter of 21.6 meters, and a front-section width of 21 meters, this tumulus revealed a surviving moat measuring 5–6.5 meters wide. Numerous human, animal, and cylindrical haniwa were recovered from the moat. Particularly, the constriction area on the mound’s eastern side displayed a dense arrangement of haniwa. - '''Mound 164 (Niwa-tori-zuka Kofun):''' At 40 meters in length, this tumulus features a box-type Sarcophagus in its constriction area. Excavations revealed straight swords, silver knives, iron arrowheads, earrings, horse fittings, and other burial goods. Cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, such as human, house, and animal figurines, were found encircling the mound. These haniwa provide critical examples of how such burial goods were arranged on Bōsō Peninsula kofun. Many round tumuli also contain box-type Sarcophaguss. The majority of excavated tumuli date to the latter half of the 6th century and exhibit characteristics of a cluster-type kofun group. == References == * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. It is distributed across a plateau rising 40–45 meters above sea level, situated between the tributaries of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River|1=Kuriyama River}}, namely the {{ill|Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|ja|高谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya River (Kuriyama River Basin)|1=Q11672847|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672847}} and the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba Prefecture)|1=Q11518414|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}}.
